game_name: Where in Time Is Carmen Sandiego?

design_philosophy.core_loop: Travel through historical time periods, gather contextual clues about a criminal's destination and identity, deduce their next location, obtain arrest warrant, capture target before time expires
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. information gathering—player must balance thorough investigation with chronometer depletion
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Detective/time-traveling secret agent solving historical mysteries by connecting clues across centuries of human history
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly educational-first design: gameplay serves pedagogy of history and geography; integrates reference materials (encyclopedia pack-in) as core mechanic rather than UI accessory; refuses action-based difficulty in favor of deduction and research literacy

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Progressive rank advancement (Time Cadet → Super Time Sleuth) likely gates case difficulty; player controls investigation pace within time limit, implying variable success paths

progression.upgrade_system: Rank/promotion system tied to successful arrests (Time Cadet → Time Patroller → Time Investigator → Time Inspector → Time Detective → Ace Time Detective → Super Time Sleuth)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Accumulative case completions (player must capture 80 criminals total to beat game); rank advancement unlocks access to new case difficulty tiers
replayability_hooks: 80 distinct criminal cases with procedurally-varied clue chains
5 language variants support replay in different languages
Rank progression encourages repeat play for skill advancement
Time-period spanning 400 AD–1950s provides educational variety

world_building_techniques: Historical period immersion—clues embedded in anachronistic details of locations spanning 1500 years
Character archetypes (VILE agents) integrated into historical locales
Encyclopedia reference material grounds fictional detective narrative in real historical events/figures
Location-specific NPC dialogue provides historical context alongside case clues

notable_bosses: Carmen Sandiego herself (final arrest target after capturing 80 subordinates)

faction_indicators: ACME Detective Agency (player affiliation, HQ established in San Francisco)
VILE organization (criminal cartel; agents serve as visual arrest confirmations at correct locations)
danger_signals: Time expiration = case failure and rank demotion
Incorrect warrant issuance = failed arrest despite correct location
environmental_storytelling: Historical period aesthetics signal location authenticity
VILE agent appearance animations confirm investigative accuracy
NPC dialogue contextualized by historical epoch and cultural references

sources_used: Wikipedia: Where in Time Is Carmen Sandiego? (video game)
